title: region — field classification
description: Region carries commercial + regulatory reference data. No PII. `tax_config.registration_number` is the highest-sensitivity field (a tenant's tax registration ID for the jurisdiction) — classified `confidential` because leakage would expose the tenant's tax posture to competitors. Every status transition + tax_config change is audit-logged (financial-record retention). Domain rows below Region (Branch, Membership, Invoice) inherit their own classification per their owning module.

regulated_financial: Region.tax_config (the aggregate object — every field participates in invoice / receipt generation, so mutations feed the tenant's financial audit trail)
Region.fiscal_year_start_month (determines when the tenant's reporting cutovers land)
audit_log: Region.is_default (every flip written to `audits` — default region change affects invoice pricing calculations)
Region.tax_config (any change written to `audits` — financial-record retention 7 years)
Region.currency (never mutable after use — but attempted changes are logged)
Region.country_code (never mutable after use — attempted changes logged)
Region.deleted_at (soft-delete + hard-delete written to `audits` + retained 7 years for financial records)

notes: `tax_config.registration_number` is the ONLY confidential field on the Region row. The redactor pipeline strips it on egress unless the caller carries the `regions.view.tax_registration` permission (admin + owner only per `permissions.json`).
Region does NOT carry any minor / health / PII data. The row is purely commercial + regulatory reference data + configuration.

The `tax_config` blob is a JSONB column. Sub-field classification above applies field-by-field when the response serialiser walks the blob. `default_rate_percent` + `name` + `inclusive` are safe for every authenticated caller (they set customer expectations); `registration_number` is not.

Financial retention (7 years) applies to Region audit rows even after the Region is hard-deleted — the audit row outlives the Region, matching the general audit-outlives-source principle (see `retention.json`).

Region.tax_config changes NEVER retroactively repricing existing Invoices. Historical Invoices carry a snapshot of the tax_config that applied at the time of issuance (finance module Wave 4 will carry that snapshot). The `region_time_travel` entitlement (Enterprise-only) unlocks retroactive edits with full audit trail.
